instruction: Go through living room, through the door on the to the right, through the den, through the dining to, to the outdoor foyer. Stop before going outside.

text: I'm orienting myself in this space—a living room with a sofa and coffee table, dining table to the left. My task is to navigate through here, into the den via a door on the right, then through the dining room to the outdoor foyer, stopping before I go outside. I notice a glass door ahead-left and an archway to the right leading to the kitchen, so I'm turning right to get a better sense of what's around me.
I turned left 90 degrees and now I'm looking at glass doors and windows on the left side with a dining nook and table visible. Let me continue rotating left to get a complete view of the space.
I'm looking at the glass door or window from the outside, then continuing to rotate left to see the remaining directions.

At 270° I'm looking at a fireplace and a glass-paneled wooden door on the left side of what appears to be a living room. This door on the left from my current perspective might correspond to the door on the right from the original orientation. So far I've mapped out the space: the original view had a sofa and coffee table with the kitchen archway to the right, then rotating left I saw the dining table and windows, then glass doors to the outside at 180°.

The wooden glass-paneled door I'm seeing now could be the entrance to the den mentioned in the instructions. I should turn a bit more to get a better look at it and head through it to continue following the path through the living room, den, dining room, and out to the outdoor foyer.

I'm orienting myself to the spatial layout—the living room has the sofa on the left, fireplace on the right, kitchen archway ahead-left, and an open wooden door with glass panes in the center leading to another room. But I'm noticing a discrepancy: in the original view, the door was positioned differently, and the instruction mentions going "through the door on the right," which doesn't quite match where the open door currently is.

Let me move forward toward that center door to see what's beyond it—the space behind looks bright, so I'll head that way to get a clearer sense of where it leads.
I'm moving forward toward the open wooden door with glass panes ahead of me, noting the kitchen visible through an archway to the left and what might be a hallway or den beyond the center door.
I'm orienting myself in this space—there's an open doorway ahead and slightly to the left, with a kitchen archway visible on the left side. I need to move forward through that doorway to continue.

I'm orienting myself in the space—there's a kitchen visible through an archway to the left, and a central doorway with darkness beyond it. The door swings outward into the living room, and I need to move forward through that opening.
I can see the open doorway leading into the living room, with a small console table and wall art visible beyond it. The doorway is slightly to the right, so I'll head through it.
I'm moving through the open double doors into a hallway where I can see a console table and a picture on the wall, with an archway visible further to the right that leads to another passage.
I'm moving through a hallway with console tables and arched openings, following the path through the den and dining room toward the outdoor foyer. I need to turn right and continue down the hall.
I'm moving through the den, which has a sofa, rug, and a bar with stools along the right side, then continuing ahead through the archways.
I'm moving through the den, noting the kitchen and bar setup to my right, then heading forward through the archways that continue down the hallway.
I'm moving through the kitchen and continuing down the hallway past the archways ahead.

I'm noticing a glass-paneled door to my left that might be exterior, with the hallway continuing ahead through arches and wooden doors on the right, but I haven't encountered the dining room yet despite the instructions pointing that way—I should continue forward to find it.
I'm pressed against a wall and need to navigate around it—let me turn right to see if the hallway continues in that direction.
I can see an arched doorway leading into the dining room with a table, chairs, and wooden ceiling. I should head through it, then turn right and continue forward.

I can see the arched doorway leading into the dining room with a table, chairs, and glass doors at the far end that might open to an outdoor area. I'll head through the arch, moving slightly to the right.
I'm moving through the dining room toward the French doors at the far end, where I can see an open doorway on the right that likely leads to the outdoor foyer beyond.

I'm in the dining room trying to navigate around the table toward an open doorway on the right side that might lead outside, but there's a sideboard blocking that route and the space looks tight, so I'm considering going around the left side of the table instead.

I'm positioned along the right side of the dining table with the buffet to my right, looking toward the far wall where French doors and a dark doorway are visible. The space between the table and buffet is narrow but passable, so I'll continue moving forward.
I'm looking at the far wall with French doors on the left and an open door on the right that leads toward the outdoor foyer. I should move forward and then slightly right through that open doorway.

I'm noticing the movement is stalled—likely blocked by the chairs in front of me. There's an open doorway visible ahead at center-right, but the chair backs are blocking my path. I'll try turning right to navigate between the chairs and the buffet instead of pushing straight forward.
I can see the French door ahead in the center with the exterior visible through it. I need to navigate around the chair and buffet to move forward.
I'm navigating around an obstacle—there's a chair blocking my path forward, so I'm planning to sidestep it by turning left, moving ahead, then turning right to get around it toward the open door.

The chair's blocking my view, so I need to find another route. I'll try backing off and moving left around the table, maybe angling toward the gap between the table's end and the far wall to get through.

I'm trying to orient myself in this space—I turned and now I'm seeing the room from a different angle, noticing the fireplace and French doors that weren't visible before. But I'm getting disoriented about the layout; the walls and openings don't quite match up with what I expected from my previous position, and I'm realizing the dining room might have more architectural complexity than I initially thought.

Wait, I need to refocus on the actual task: I'm supposed to stop at the outdoor foyer before going outside, and that open door with the dark opening is my target. Let me reorient myself and turn back to face that door again.
I'm back at the open doorway with the dark passage beyond it, and the gap between the chairs and buffet is straight ahead. I've moved closer, so I'll try advancing forward again.
I've moved forward and the open exterior door is now to my left. The buffet sits on my right side. I need to turn slightly left and move through the gap ahead to reach that open door.

I'm stuck with the chair blocking my view forward, so I'm considering going around the left side of the table toward the French doors instead. But I'm also noticing there might be a separate dark doorway on the right side near the buffet—I need to figure out if that's actually a hallway entrance or something else.

Let me try a different approach: angle left about 30 degrees, move forward a couple steps, then angle right 30 degrees and continue forward to see if that gets me past this obstruction.

I've gotten turned around and need to navigate around the table instead. The chairs are blocking the right path, so I'll go left—turning about 60 degrees to get the table on my right side, then walking past its end before turning right toward the open door.

I'm orienting myself in the space—the dining room connects to a larger living area with a fireplace, and there's an open exterior door that likely leads to the outdoor foyer. Since the instruction is to stop before going outside, I need to pause at that threshold rather than step through it.

Let me recalibrate my position and approach the open doorway more directly. I was roughly 2-3 meters away when I got stuck at the chairs, so I should navigate around the left side of the table to get closer to that open door and reach the foyer entrance.

I'm still at the same bottleneck between the chair and buffet, with the dark doorway ahead. The chair is jutting out from the bottom-left, and the buffet corner blocks the right side, so I need to navigate carefully through the narrow gap by hugging the buffet—I'll try turning right 15 degrees, moving forward one unit, then turning left to slip through.
I'm getting closer to the door on my left with the buffet to the right and a chair on the left side—moving slightly left and forward.

The open door panel is right in front of me, swung into the room. I can see the actual doorway opening to the left and behind it—that dark gap between the French door sets. There's another dark rectangular opening to the right that might lead to a different room. The exterior opening appears to be at the center-left, between the left French doors and the open panel, with light visible below the dark gap—I should move slightly left and forward toward it.

I've rotated and can now see the table on my left with French doors ahead—the open doorway is center-right with exterior light visible through it, and there's a chair blocking my direct path forward. I'm trying to figure out if I can move ahead or if the furniture is in the way.

Looking more carefully at the floor and rug ahead toward center-right, it actually looks clear enough to move forward.
